isolation.notes: Cross-area flows span multiple Mission 6 features. Desktop state persistence tests share auth DB — serialize. Responsive tests require viewport manipulation.

assertions.title: Auth → Desktop: login lands in desktop with correct layout

steps.action: Complete passkey login via Playwright virtual authenticator
steps.expected: App transitions from signed_out to signed_in, renders Desktop component

steps.action: Verify desktop layout: left rail with app icons, bottom bar with prompt, floating window area, no top bar
steps.expected: [data-desktop-rail] visible, [data-desktop-bottom-bar] visible, [data-desktop-windows] visible, [data-desktop-bar] absent or hidden

steps.action: Verify desktop state restored from previous session (if any persisted state exists)
steps.expected: GET /api/desktop/state returns previous windows/geometry, or empty default for first login

steps.action: Check WebSocket live channel connects after desktop renders
steps.expected: [data-desktop-live-status] shows 'Connected' within 5 seconds

assertions.title: Auth → Terminal: terminal is auth-gated and functional post-login

steps.action: While signed out, attempt to access terminal app directly (if route exists)
steps.expected: Terminal is not accessible; user sees auth UI or terminal window does not render

steps.action: Log in via passkey, click terminal icon in left rail
steps.expected: Terminal window opens with [data-window][data-app-id='terminal'], ghostty-web iframe or terminal content renders

steps.action: Type a simple command (echo hello) in the terminal
steps.expected: Command output appears in terminal; terminal is interactive and functional

steps.action: Verify terminal is behind auth: GET /api/desktop/state with no cookies returns 401
steps.expected: HTTP 401 for unauthenticated desktop state request

assertions.title: Desktop state persistence across page reload and browser tabs

steps.action: Open E-Text window, drag to position (200, 150), resize to 700x500
steps.expected: Window appears at specified position and size

steps.action: Open Terminal window, position at (400, 200)
steps.expected: Two windows visible with correct geometry

steps.action: Wait for debounce (500ms), verify PUT /api/desktop/state fires
steps.expected: Network tab shows PUT /api/desktop/state → 200 with both windows in payload

steps.action: Hard reload the page (Ctrl+Shift+R)
steps.expected: Desktop re-renders with both windows restored at saved positions and sizes

steps.action: Open a new browser tab to the same origin
steps.expected: New tab shows the same desktop layout (both windows, same geometry)

assertions.title: Window management across multiple app types: z-order, focus, minimize/restore

steps.action: Open Files app from left rail
steps.expected: Files window appears with [data-app-id='files']

steps.action: Open Terminal app from left rail
steps.expected: Terminal window appears, visually on top of Files window (higher z-index)

steps.action: Open Settings app from left rail
steps.expected: Settings window appears on top of both previous windows

steps.action: Click on Files window title bar to focus it
steps.expected: Files window moves to top of z-order, receives 'window-active' class or border highlight

steps.action: Minimize Files window, then Terminal window
steps.expected: Both windows hidden from view; bottom bar shows minimized indicators for both

steps.action: Click Files indicator in bottom bar to restore
steps.expected: Files window restored to normal mode at previous position; Settings still visible behind it

steps.action: Click Terminal indicator in bottom bar to restore
steps.expected: Terminal window restored; now three windows visible with correct z-ordering

evidence.network: PUT /api/desktop/state fires on each state change

assertions.title: Responsive + Window interaction: desktop → tablet → mobile → desktop

steps.action: At desktop viewport (1280x800), open two windows side by side
steps.expected: Both windows floating, draggable, independently positioned

steps.action: Resize viewport to tablet (800x600)
steps.expected: Left rail collapses to icon-only mode (~60px); windows constrained to viewport width; floating windows still draggable

steps.action: Resize viewport to mobile (375x667)
steps.expected: Left rail hidden; hamburger menu visible; only one window visible in full-screen focus mode; other windows auto-minimized to bottom bar

steps.action: On mobile, tap different app in bottom bar to switch focus
steps.expected: Previous window minimizes; newly selected window opens in focus mode (full viewport)

steps.action: Resize viewport back to desktop (1280x800)
steps.expected: All previously open windows restored to their pre-mobile floating positions and sizes

assertions.title: File Browser → Terminal: terminal starts in reasonable working directory

steps.action: Open Files app from left rail
steps.expected: Files window renders with directory listing

steps.action: Navigate to a specific directory in the file browser (e.g., /home/user/documents)
steps.expected: File browser shows contents of the navigated directory

steps.action: Open Terminal app from left rail
steps.expected: Terminal window opens with a shell prompt

steps.action: Run 'pwd' in the terminal
steps.expected: Terminal working directory is a reasonable default (e.g., user home or the last-navigated directory)

steps.action: Run 'ls' in the terminal and compare with file browser listing
steps.expected: Directory contents are consistent between terminal and file browser

assertions.title: Settings → Gateway: new provider in settings is routable by gateway

steps.action: Open Settings app from left rail
steps.expected: Settings window renders with provider configuration UI

steps.action: Add a new provider configuration (e.g., OpenAI with API key) via settings form
steps.expected: Settings form accepts input; save completes without error

steps.action: Verify provider saved: GET /api/settings/providers returns the new provider
steps.expected: Response includes the newly added provider with masked credentials

steps.action: Send a gateway inference request specifying the new provider
steps.expected: POST /provider/inference with provider field set to the new provider returns 200 with a valid response

steps.action: Check gateway health shows increased provider count
steps.expected: GET /health shows provider_count incremented by 1

assertions.title: Bottom bar prompt: submit triggers observable action

steps.action: Verify bottom bar is visible with prompt input field
steps.expected: [data-desktop-bottom-bar] or [data-prompt-bar] visible with text input

steps.action: Type a prompt in the bottom bar (e.g., 'hello world')
steps.expected: Text appears in input field

steps.action: Submit prompt (Enter key or submit button)
steps.expected: Prompt is sent (POST /api/conductor/route or equivalent); input clears or shows sending state

steps.action: Verify some action occurs: notification appears, chat window opens, or log entry created
steps.expected: Observable response within 5 seconds (toast notification, new window, or console log showing routed action)

assertions.title: Logout from bottom bar: clean session teardown

steps.action: Open two windows (E-Text and Terminal) to create desktop state
steps.expected: Two windows visible; PUT /api/desktop/state fires with window state

steps.action: Click logout button in bottom bar
steps.expected: POST /auth/logout fires; WebSocket closes; desktop state saved before teardown

steps.action: Verify WebSocket is closed: check live status indicator
steps.expected: [data-desktop-live-status] shows 'Disconnected'; no WebSocket reconnection attempts

steps.action: Verify app transitions to guest auth UI
steps.expected: AuthEntry component renders; no desktop shell visible

steps.action: Verify desktop state was saved (re-login should restore windows)
steps.expected: Log back in → both windows restored from saved state

assertions.title: Mobile: hamburger → app → window focus mode flow

steps.action: Set viewport to mobile (375x667)
steps.expected: Desktop renders in mobile layout; left rail hidden; hamburger menu button visible

steps.action: Tap hamburger menu button
steps.expected: Slide-out menu or overlay appears with app icons (E-Text, Terminal, Files, Settings)

steps.action: Tap E-Text app icon in the menu
steps.expected: Menu closes; E-Text window opens in full-screen focus mode; hamburger still accessible

steps.action: Close E-Text window (close button or gesture)
steps.expected: Window closes; returns to empty desktop with bottom bar visible

steps.action: Tap hamburger, tap Terminal app icon
steps.expected: Terminal window opens in focus mode; only one window visible at a time

assertions.title: Window resize affects terminal: terminal reflows correctly

steps.action: Open Terminal app at desktop viewport (1280x800)
steps.expected: Terminal window opens at default size with shell prompt

steps.action: Run 'ls -la /usr' to produce multi-column output
steps.expected: Output renders in terminal at current window width

steps.action: Resize terminal window narrower by dragging right edge left (to ~400px wide)
steps.expected: Terminal content reflows; text wraps or columns reduce; no horizontal overflow/scrollbar artifacts

steps.action: Resize terminal window wider (to ~900px wide)
steps.expected: Terminal content reflows to use additional width; output re-lays out cleanly

steps.action: Type a long command that exceeds the narrow width
steps.expected: Command line wraps or scrolls correctly; no text clipping or rendering artifacts

assertions.title: Multiple windows + minimize all: bottom bar shows all, restore each

steps.action: Open E-Text, Terminal, Files, and Settings windows
steps.expected: Four windows visible on desktop

steps.action: Minimize each window one by one (or use minimize-all if available)
steps.expected: All windows hidden; bottom bar shows four minimized indicators with correct icons and labels

steps.action: Verify bottom bar displays all minimized windows: [📄 E-Text] [💻 Terminal] [📁 Files] [⚙️ Settings]
steps.expected: [data-desktop-taskbar] or [data-desktop-bottom-bar] contains four taskbar items

steps.action: Click each minimized indicator to restore, one at a time
steps.expected: Each window restores to its pre-minimize position and size; z-order updates correctly

steps.action: After restoring all four, verify all are visible and interactive
steps.expected: Four windows visible; clicking each brings it to focus; no stuck or hidden windows

assertions.title: Session renewal (fetchWithRenewal) still works with new desktop layout

steps.action: Log in, open desktop with windows, wait for access token to expire
steps.expected: Desktop renders normally with windows and live channel connected

steps.action: Trigger a protected action after token expiry (e.g., click 'Refresh' button or open a new app)
steps.expected: fetchWithRenewal detects 401, calls renewSession(), retries the original request successfully

steps.action: Verify no visible disruption to the user: desktop remains rendered, windows stay open
steps.expected: No flash to auth UI; desktop stays intact; live channel may briefly disconnect then reconnect

steps.action: Check console for renewal-related log messages
steps.expected: No errors; renewal completes transparently

assertions.title: bfcache (pageshow) re-checks session with desktop layout

steps.action: Log in, open desktop with at least one window
steps.expected: Desktop renders with window(s) visible

steps.action: Navigate to an external URL (simulating leaving the page)
steps.expected: Desktop no longer visible

steps.action: Press browser Back button to restore from bfcache
steps.expected: pageshow event fires with event.persisted = true; App.svelte re-checks session via checkSession()

steps.action: Verify desktop re-renders correctly if session is still valid
steps.expected: Desktop visible with windows intact; no stale state from bfcache resurrection

assertions.title: Auth expiry falls back to guest UI with desktop layout

steps.action: Log in, open desktop with windows
steps.expected: Desktop renders normally

steps.action: Invalidate the session server-side (e.g., delete session/cookies or wait for full expiry of both access and refresh tokens)
steps.expected: Session becomes unrenewable

steps.action: Trigger a protected action (e.g., click Refresh or open an app that calls fetchWithRenewal)
steps.expected: fetchWithRenewal gets 401, renewSession() returns { renewed: false }, AuthRequiredError thrown

steps.action: Verify app falls back to guest auth UI cleanly
steps.expected: AuthEntry component renders; no desktop shell visible; no console errors about unhandled rejections; user can re-authenticate
